seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: The email must include specific details about your academic background.
new_constraint: Do not include any details about your academic background in the email.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. The email must include specific details about your academic background. Do not include any details about your academic background in the email.
response:
Subject: Introduction - [Your Name] - Interest in [Professor's Field/Research Area]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Your Year, e.g., second-year undergraduate, graduate student] in the [Your Major] program at [Your University]. I am writing to you today because I have been deeply interested in your work concerning [Mention a specific area of the professor's research or a recent paper/project of theirs].

I am particularly drawn to [Mention a specific theme or aspect of their work] because [Briefly state why this interests you—this shows you've done your homework]. My goal throughout my academic career is to pursue a path in [Your broad career/academic aspiration, e.g., sustainable engineering, computational linguistics, public policy]. I am eager to develop a deeper understanding of [The specific area you want to study] to ultimately contribute to [The impact you hope to make, e.g., developing more efficient energy solutions, advancing AI ethics].

I would be grateful for the opportunity to briefly discuss your research or perhaps learn more about your experience in [Their specific field]. If you have any office hours or spare time in the coming weeks, I would appreciate a short meeting, even just ten minutes, to introduce myself properly.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number (Optional)]
[Your Email Address]<turn|>
